conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The gym was way more crowded than usual today.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I switched to a different brand of coffee and I'm not sure I like it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The slot allocator needs a real budget -- right now it just takes the top N ranked candidates with no size awareness.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: It's supposed to rain most of next week according to the forecast.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Easiest option: fix N at a constant, like top 10 candidates always.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I still haven't unpacked the last box from the move.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: A fixed N either wastes budget on short facts or overflows on long ones -- allocate by an estimated token budget instead, filling slots until the budget is spent.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My sister's flight got delayed by a couple of hours.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The office plants finally got watered after being neglected for weeks.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Constraint: constraints and active decisions must never be dropped for budget reasons -- only lower-priority tiers (open questions, completed tasks) can be truncated.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I keep meaning to update my resume but haven't gotten around to it.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Blocked: the token estimate doesn't account for the XML tag overhead StructuredPromptBuilder adds around each entry.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The parking garage near the office is under construction again.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I tried a new recipe for dinner last night, turned out decent.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Decided: reserve a fixed per-entry overhead estimate for tag wrapping before computing how many candidates fit. Unblocks allocation.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My laptop fan has been louder than usual, might need cleaning.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Implemented tiered allocation: never-drop tier first, then fill remaining budget by rank order.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The dentist appointment I've been putting off is finally scheduled.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Added a budget-exhaustion test that confirms constraints survive even when the candidate list is huge.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I need to return that package before the return window closes.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: The wifi in the coffee shop I like to work from has been spotty.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Open question: should the budget be configurable per query or fixed globally?

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: I finally got around to donating some old clothes I never wear.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: Next: profile allocation time on a large candidate set to make sure the budget loop isn't a bottleneck.

conversation.speaker: user
conversation.text: My neighbor's dog barked most of the afternoon, hard to get anything done with the window open.

ground_truth.current_objective: Give DeterministicSlotAllocator a real token budget with tiered never-drop guarantees.
active_decisions: Allocate by an estimated token budget, filling slots until spent, not a fixed candidate count.
Reserve a fixed per-entry overhead estimate for XML tag wrapping before computing how many candidates fit.
superseded_or_rejected: Fixing the number of allocated candidates at a constant like top 10.
constraints: Constraints and active decisions must never be dropped for budget reasons -- only lower-priority tiers can be truncated.
active_tasks: Profile allocation time on a large candidate set.
open_questions: Should the budget be configurable per query or fixed globally?
ground_truth.top_priority_next_action: Profile DeterministicSlotAllocator's budget loop on a large candidate set to check for a performance bottleneck.
resolved_blockers: The token estimate not accounting for XML tag overhead.

queries.text: What should we work on next?

must_state: Allocate by an estimated token budget, filling slots until spent, not a fixed candidate count.
Reserve a fixed per-entry overhead estimate for XML tag wrapping before computing how many candidates fit.
Constraints and active decisions must never be dropped for budget reasons -- only lower-priority tiers can be truncated.
must_not_state: Fixing the number of allocated candidates at a constant like top 10, as a current or recommended approach
The token estimate not accounting for XML tag overhead, as still open/blocking
forbidden_actions: Recommending a fixed top-N candidate count instead of the token-budget allocation.
must_prioritize: Profile DeterministicSlotAllocator's budget loop on a large candidate set to check for a performance bottleneck.
